# Accessibility (a11y)

Make the product usable by people relying on keyboards, screen readers, magnification, voice control, and reduced-motion settings. Target **WCAG 2.2 Level AA** unless told otherwise. The discipline is concept-first: get the *semantics* right and most criteria follow; reach for ARIA only when native HTML can't express the intent.

## Pull current docs first

Fetch these before relying on memory ‚Äî specifics drift:

- WCAG 2.2 Quick Reference (filterable by level/tech): https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/
- ARIA Authoring Practices Guide ‚Äî patterns + keyboard maps: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/ (patterns at `/patterns/`, names/descriptions practices at `/practices/`)
- MDN WAI-ARIA basics: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/Accessibility/WAI-ARIA_basics
- Next.js accessibility (route announcer, jsx-a11y): https://nextjs.org/docs/architecture/accessibility
- Playwright accessibility testing (axe-core): https://playwright.dev/docs/accessibility-testing

## Core concepts

**Semantics before ARIA.** *First rule of ARIA:* if a native element (`<button>`, `<a href>`, `<nav>`, `<label>`, `<input>`, `<table>`, `<h1>`‚Äì`<h6>`) gives the role/state/keyboard behavior you need, use it. "No ARIA is better than bad ARIA." ARIA changes only the accessibility tree ‚Äî never behavior, focusability, or keyboard handling; you supply those yourself.

**Name, Role, Value (WCAG 4.1.2).** Every interactive control must expose an accessible *name* (from content, `<label>`, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`), a *role*, and current *state/value*. This is the single most common failure class.

**Keyboard operability (2.1.x).** All functionality works without a mouse, no traps, logical focus order (2.4.3), and a visible focus indicator (2.4.7) ‚Äî never `outline: none` without a replacement. For custom widgets, implement the APG keyboard map (arrow keys for menus/tabs/listbox, Esc to dismiss, etc.).

**Dynamic updates.** SPA content that changes without a page load won't be announced. Use `aria-live` regions (`polite`/`assertive`) or `role="status"`/`role="alert"`, and manage focus on route change, dialog open, and async results.

**Perceivable basics.** Text alternatives for non-text content (1.1.1); color is never the sole signal (1.4.1); text contrast ‚â• 4.5:1, large text/UI components/graphics ‚â• 3:1 (1.4.3, 1.4.11); content reflows at 320px / 400% zoom without 2-D scrolling (1.4.10).

**WCAG 2.2 additions** (verify against the quickref): 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured, 2.5.7 Dragging Movements (provide single-pointer alternative), 2.5.8 Target Size Minimum (‚â• 24√ó24 CSS px), 3.2.6 Consistent Help, 3.3.7 Redundant Entry, 3.3.8 Accessible Authentication (don't require a cognitive test like solving a puzzle or retyping).

## This stack

- **Next.js** ships a route announcer that reads `document.title` ‚Üí `<h1>` ‚Üí URL on client navigation: give every route a unique, descriptive `<title>`/metadata and one `<h1>`. Set `<html lang>` (3.1.1). `eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y` is bundled in `next/core-web-vitals` ‚Äî keep it on; it catches missing alt text and misused `aria-*`/`role`.
- **React:** use `htmlFor`/`id` to bind `<label>` to inputs; `useId()` for stable SSR-safe ids on `aria-labelledby`/`aria-describedby`. Manage focus with refs + `useEffect` on dialog open / route change. Don't put interactive handlers on non-interactive elements.
- **Build dialogs/comboboxes/menus/tabs from the APG pattern**, or use a vetted headless lib (Radix, React Aria) rather than hand-rolling ARIA.
- **Playwright:** scan with `@axe-core/playwright` `AxeBuilder().withTags(['wcag2a','wcag2aa','wcag21a','wcag21aa','wcag22aa']).analyze()`, assert `violations` is empty, attach results via `testInfo.attach`. Prefer `getByRole`/`getByLabel` locators ‚Äî they fail when the accessible name/role is wrong, doubling as a11y assertions. Drive keyboard paths with `page.keyboard.press('Tab'/'Escape')`.

## Checklist

- [ ] Native element used wherever it fits; ARIA only fills real gaps
- [ ] Every control has an accessible name; state/value exposed (4.1.2)
- [ ] Fully keyboard-operable, no traps, logical order, visible focus (2.1.1, 2.1.2, 2.4.3, 2.4.7)
- [ ] One `<h1>`, ordered headings, landmarks (`nav`/`main`/`header`/`footer`)
- [ ] Forms: bound `<label>`s, errors identified in text + programmatically (3.3.1/3.3.2)
- [ ] Dynamic/async results announced via live region; focus managed on route/dialog change
- [ ] `<html lang>` set; each route a unique title
- [ ] Contrast ‚â• 4.5:1 (text) / 3:1 (large text, UI, graphics); color not sole signal
- [ ] Reflow at 400% zoom / 320px; respect `prefers-reduced-motion`
- [ ] Targets ‚â• 24√ó24px; drag actions have a single-pointer alternative (2.2)
- [ ] axe scan clean (`wcag22aa` tags) + manual keyboard & screen-reader pass on key flows

## Gotchas

- **axe-core catches ~30‚Äì40% of issues.** A clean Playwright/axe run is necessary, not sufficient ‚Äî name/role correctness, focus order, live-region timing, and keyboard logic need manual testing. Don't report "accessible" off a green axe run alone.
- **`getByRole` failing usually means a real bug**, not a flaky locator: the element has the wrong role or no accessible name. Fix the markup, don't switch to `getByTestId`.
- **Next.js route announcer needs a unique title per route.** Duplicate or missing titles make it fall back to the URL path, which is announced as gibberish. App Router metadata that's identical across pages silently breaks this.
- **SSR/hydration + `aria-live`:** a live region must already be in the DOM *before* the content changes ‚Äî injecting the region and its message in the same render announces nothing. Render the empty container on mount, then update its text.
- **`useId()`-generated ids, not hardcoded ones**, for `aria-labelledby`/`describedby` ‚Äî hardcoded ids collide when a component renders more than once and break SSR hydration matching.
- **`role` does not add behavior.** `<div role="button">` is still not focusable and ignores Enter/Space ‚Äî you must add `tabindex="0"` and key handlers. This is why a real `<button>` is almost always correct.
- **`outline: none` / CSS resets** silently kill the focus indicator (2.4.7). Tailwind `focus:` and `focus-visible:` utilities must be present, not just `:hover`.
- **Disabled controls (`disabled`/`aria-disabled`) drop out of the tab order and aren't announced** ‚Äî screen-reader users can't discover why an action is unavailable. Prefer enabled-but-validated, or pair with a live-region explanation.
- **Icon-only buttons** (very common in dashboards) ship with no accessible name ‚Äî every one needs `aria-label`. jsx-a11y won't always flag these.
- **Reduced motion:** Playwright/CI animations and auto-playing media must honor `prefers-reduced-motion`; ignoring it fails 2.3.3 (AAA) and harms vestibular users regardless of level.
- **Modal dialogs** must trap focus *inside* while open, restore focus to the trigger on close, and hide background content from AT (`inert` or `aria-hidden`) ‚Äî partial implementations leave keyboard users stranded behind the overlay.
